That on Christmas day, 1860, the
only people Of Spokane who cele-
brated Christmas were on old buck
Indian, his sQuaw, three papooses
and an old pinto cayuse; and they
went fishing?


That Stevens county, although
more cut up than any other county
of the state, is still the largest?


That near Daisy, WASH, In the
PinIfSok country, there still stands
the cr,tnbling remains of an old log
"block house." which was much
used in the early days when all
freight for the northern part of the
Inland Empire was hauled in wag-
ons from Fort Walla Walla.


That Waite's lake, a few miles
from Valley. Wash, and right on
top of a mountain, is supposed to
be the crater Of an extinct volcano?


That the Blackfoot Indians Of
Montana and the Dakotas once
came as far west as Republic.
Wash, on raiding expeditions?


That Hillyard had newspaper
more than IA years ago? II was
called the Hillyard Headlight, and
It soon went out.
